channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Virtual hospital 'won't drain staff from other providers'
item.description: NHS Online will implement “safeguards” to prevent it from “draining the resources” of the wider NHS workforce.

item.title: Knighthood for hospital chief
item.description: A trust group CEO currently working as NHS England’s deputy chief executive has been awarded a knighthood in this year’s King’s birthday honours, for services to the NHS.

item.title: Mapped: RTT waiting times
item.description: An interactive map of local NHS waits around England in April 2026, showing the pressures, with links to all the details by organisation and specialty.

item.title: Trust labelled ‘inadequate’ amid ‘serious safety concerns’
item.description: A trust has had its leadership rating downgraded from “good” to “inadequate”, and been told it has “significant and serious safety concerns” in surgical services.

item.title: Biggest climbers and fallers in trust league table
item.description: Sharply divergent performance in the last quarter of 2025-26 saw dramatic movement within NHS England’s league table for acute trusts, with six providers moving up or down rankings by 30 places or more.

item.title: Take more risks with new technology urges health secretary
item.description: The new health secretary has promised to “back” NHS leaders who take risks by increasing the use of new technology, in his first major speech in the role

item.title: ICB exec steps back from consultancy firm founded with former CEO
item.description: An integrated care board executive has stepped back from a management consultancy firm she helped to found with the former chief executive of the ICB, just days after its launch.

item.title: Corridor care cases hit 90k in May
item.description: More than 90,000 cases of corridor care were recorded in English NHS hospitals in May – an average of 3,000 per day – according to the first official stats on the scale of the problem.

item.title: Revealed: The trusts sharing £86m prize for March A&E sprint
item.description: NHS England has revealed the trusts which will share an £86m prize pot for delivering improvements to A&E waiting times in March.

item.title: Digital chief: NHS AI ban is ‘criminal’
item.description: No NHS staff are allowed to use common AI tools – which is “criminal” and encouraging “shadow” workarounds, a senior digital chief has said.

item.title: ‘Innovation freeze’ threatening NHSE AI plans
item.description: NHS England has warned that it may be unable to lawfully deploy AI features on the NHS App from next year, due to incoming medical device regulation changes.

item.title: Long-serving deputy appointed acute trust’s CEO
item.description: An acute trust’s deputy chief executive has been appointed its next CEO, following a decade serving on the board.

item.title: Trust boards told to ‘grip’ cyber security
item.description: Trust boards must demonstrate they have “grip” of their cyber security, NHS England said.

item.title: Revealed: Hospitals with the most ‘red line’ 24-hour waits
item.description: At least one in 10 A&E patients wait more than 24 hours at many hospitals, despite NHS England telling trusts to adopt a “zero tolerance” approach to such long waits, new figures have revealed.

item.title: Men earn more at 89% of trusts
item.description: The typical man earned 8 per cent more than the typical woman at nearly nine in 10 trusts last year, new figures show.

item.title: Ministers to consider scrapping separate national NHS CEO
item.description: The post of NHS chief executive could be abolished or combined with that of permanent secretary when NHSE England is merged into the Department of Health and Social Care, under proposals being considered by officials, HSJ understands.

item.title: First ‘neighbourhood contract’ links GP income to A&E attendances
item.description: Some £1.7m of primary care revenue will be tied to A&E attendances from next year, under a first-of-its-kind “neighbourhood” contract deal.

item.title: Exclusive: Government plans US-style ‘hospitalist’ doctors
item.description: The government will introduce a new class of generalist hospital doctors – inspired by the American “hospitalist” role – by the end of the decade, according to leaked details of its upcoming workforce plan.

item.title: Government will be ‘incredibly lucky’ if current elective care plan works
item.description: The government and NHS England is wrong to think that improvements in productivity, and not increased funding, will result in shorter elective waiting lists, the leader of the NHS Alliance has claimed.

item.title: CEO departs trust with leadership ‘disconnect’
item.description: A trust grappling with concerns about leadership and culture has announced the retirement of its CEO.
